steps.title: Identify what the question requires
steps.content: The student must compare the causes of crown shyness proposed in the two theories. The notes identify two different mechanisms: Jacobs says branches brush and break, while Ng says branches detect shade and stop growing.

steps.title: Apply the requirement to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A directly compares both causes using a contrast structure (“While Jacobs proposes...

Ng posits...”), providing each theory's specific mechanism.

The other three lack this comparison—they're either generic, mention only one theory, or omit Ng.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
